testing_updates.highlight: SSZ support from project ZKEVM being integrated into EELS Amsterdam branch; reference vectors and REST abstraction to follow
EELS planned to eventually serve engine requests as a temporary EL for CL testing; FastAPI/OpenRPC targeted short-term

ssz_engine_api_design.highlight: Jacek (Nimbus) proposes REST path alignment with Beacon API namespace for coexistence on same port; especially relevant for unified clients
Top-up sync gap identified: current Engine API has no reliable way for a CL to determine where to begin backfilling to an out-of-sync EL head
Proposal: expose ETH get-latest-canonical-block (FCU-marked head) on engine port to enable CL-driven top-up sync without EL p2p
SSZ REST Engine API currently lacks equivalents for ETH-namespace methods (chainID, syncing, etc.) that existing JSON-RPC engine spec guarantees; needs explicit scoping

action_items.action: Prepare document explaining current ETH-namespace method list on engine port, rationale, and candidates for removal; bring to ACDE and ACDC for broader input
Publish public document on top-up sync design (incorporating Jacek's HackMD and chain-following mechanism) to gather input from absent clients

decisions.decision: Agreed SSZ Engine API must specify which ETH-namespace endpoints are available on the engine port, consistent with Beacon API encoding conventions
ETH-namespace method list for SSZ Engine API should be as minimal as possible; no automatic carry-over from JSON-RPC engine spec

targets.target: Next SSZ Engine API call in 2 weeks (approx. July 24, 2026); top-up sync and namespace scoping documents to be circulated beforehand
